name: Waialae Iki Ridge
desc: Ocean-view ridge community of roughly 600 homes — elevated above Kahala, quiet cul-de-sacs, well-kept family enclave.
insider: The ocean views from the upper cul-de-sacs on Kaialii Place and Makalika Street are genuinely panoramic — Diamond Head to Koko Head in one sweep
About 600 homes total, which is small enough that most residents recognize each other at the mailbox or walking the dog
Morning fog rolls through from the Koolau side and typically burns off by 9am, revealing spectacular coastline views — it's like a daily magic trick
The single access road connects down to Kalanianaole Highway, putting you 5 minutes from Hawaii Kai shopping or 10 minutes from Kahala Mall
HOA keeps the streets clean and landscaping maintained — this is a neighborhood where pride of ownership is visibly high
There are no sidewalks on most streets, so morning joggers and dog walkers share the road with cars — drive slowly, especially on blind curves
The Wiliwilinui Ridge Trail trailhead is accessible from Waialae Iki, making it one of the rare neighborhoods where a world-class hike starts at your doorstep
Homes range from $1.2M to $3M+ depending on elevation and view — upper lots command a premium but deliver genuinely better panoramas
Trade winds hit the ridge consistently, keeping temperatures comfortable even in summer when lower elevations swelter
The neighborhood is dead quiet after 8pm — there is virtually no nightlife, no through traffic, and no commercial noise of any kind
